title: Deliciously Bitter Ethiopian Chinese Leaf Injera
description: A traditional Ethiopian dish with a Chinese twist, featuring bitter Chinese leaf and a miso marinade, perfect for a hangover cure and diabetic-friendly, served in the form of injera.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the chopped Chinese leaf and miso paste, and let it sit for 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the teff flour, water, yeast, and salt to make the injera batter.
Add the chopped bitter melon to the injera batter and stir well.
Heat a non-stick skillet or injera pan over medium heat.
Pour a small amount of the injera batter into the skillet and tilt to evenly coat the bottom.
Cook the injera for 1-2 minutes, until the edges start to curl and the surface is dry.
Brush the cooked injera with olive oil and top with the Chinese leaf and miso mixture.
Repeat the process until all the batter is used up.
Serve the injera hot, garnished with additional chopped Chinese leaf if desired.
